methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
HLabs is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Paul Hetherington that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws from a broader comp universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable, though the immediate peer set at seed stage in this sector is limited.

Given the scarcity of direct seed-stage comps in Robotics & Manufacturing, only the Stage-Progression method proved applicable. This approach applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $5M total raised as a proxy for current value, yielding an implied valuation of $12M. The Peer-Median method could not be applied; while 11 same-sector peers exist overall, none met the narrow filters for same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with sufficient datapoints (minimum 3 required), making a sector-wide median potentially unrepresentative of seed-stage risk and stage dynamics.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $14M (high), with a median of $12M. However, this range rests on a single methodology and should be weighted accordingly. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct peer benchmarks at seed stage, which limits confidence in the step-up multiplier itself, and the heterogeneity of the broader Robotics & Manufacturing sector. Additional validation through customer traction, technical differentiation, or team experience would strengthen conviction in this range.
